user_story: As an infrastructure operator, I want a single overview of my CMDB estate so that I can gauge inventory size, health, and risk at a glance.
expected_behavior: Fetches ciService.getDashboardStats() via react-query (staleTime 60s, refetchInterval 5min); shows a spinner while loading and an ErrorBanner on failure. Renders four clickable KPI MetricCards (total CIs -> /cis, active CIs -> /cis?status=active, health score % -> /cmdb-health, critical relationships -> /anomalies) plus a status pie, environment pie, and a top-10 CI-type bar chart built from stats.by_status/by_environment/by_type. Header actions navigate to /discovery (Run discovery) and /cis (Inventory).

user_story: As a CMDB editor, I want to browse, search, and filter all configuration items so that I can find and inspect specific CIs.
expected_behavior: Renders the shared CIList component, seeding its type/status/environment/search filters from URL query params (?type=&status=&environment=&search=). Clicking a row calls handleView -> navigate(`/cis/${ci.id}`). showActions is false so row-level CRUD is hidden. A 'Manage CIs' button in the header navigates to /inventory for create/edit flows.
issue_note: The 'Manage CIs' button navigates to /inventory (CIList.tsx:40) but no /inventory <Route> is mounted in App.tsx, so the catch-all '*' route redirects the user to the Dashboard instead.

user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to view the full detail of a single configuration item so that I can review its attributes, relationships, and history.
expected_behavior: Reads ciId from the route params; if absent it renders a destructive Alert ('Invalid CI ID'). Otherwise it renders the shared CIDetail component passing onBack (-> /cis), onEdit, and onDelete callbacks. Edit and Delete both navigate to /inventory.
issue_note: handleEdit and handleDelete navigate to /inventory (CIDetail.tsx:24,29) but /inventory has no route in App.tsx, so editing/deleting a CI dead-ends at the Dashboard via the '*' fallback.

user_story: As a discovery engineer, I want to run and monitor infrastructure discovery so that CIs are scanned and cataloged from cloud, server, and network sources.
expected_behavior: Tabbed UI (Dashboard, Definitions, Ad Hoc, Jobs) with the active tab synced to the ?tab= query param. Uses the useDiscoveryDefinitions hook to load/create/update/delete/run definitions and enable/disable schedules; Definitions tab wires those handlers, Ad Hoc triggers one-off jobs, Jobs lists runs. A help dialog explains Discovery vs Connectors and offers a button navigating to /connectors.

user_story: As an operator, I want to see my deployed discovery agents so that I can confirm they are online and healthy.
expected_behavior: Thin page wrapper that renders the AgentDashboard component, which lists registered discovery agents and their connection/health status. All data fetching and interaction logic lives inside AgentDashboard.

user_story: As a security admin, I want to manage discovery and connector credentials in one place so that scans and integrations can authenticate securely.
expected_behavior: Loads a paginated list via credentialService.listCredentials({limit,page,protocol}) on mount and whenever page/rows/protocol/category change; category (discovery vs connector) is filtered client-side by protocol. Create/Edit/Detail/Delete are driven by dialog state: handleCreate, handleEdit (updates name/description/credentials/tags), handleDelete, and openDetail/openEdit/openDelete first fetch the full credential via getCredential(id). Each action shows a success or error toast and reloads the list.

user_story: As a security admin, I want to group credentials into sets with selection strategies so that discovery jobs can pick the right credential by context.
expected_behavior: Renders the CredentialSetList component, which (via credential.service) lists, creates, edits, and deletes credential sets at /credential-sets and resolves a credential through POST /credential-sets/:id/select using context + strategy. The route is registered in App.tsx and is functional when reached.
issue_note: /credential-sets is an orphan route: it is absent from Sidebar.tsx menuCategories and no page (including Credentials.tsx) links to it, so it is only reachable by typing the URL directly.

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want to configure and monitor connectors so that I can sync data bi-directionally with external ITSM and monitoring platforms.
expected_behavior: Tabbed UI (Dashboard, Definitions, Jobs) with the active tab synced to ?tab=. Renders ConnectorDashboard, ConnectorDefinitionList, and ConnectorJobList for the respective tabs, plus a help dialog. Connector definition and job data is loaded inside those child components.

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want to browse and install connectors from a registry so that I can add new integrations without manual setup.
expected_behavior: Uses react-query to load the connector registry and installed connectors. Supports grid/list view toggle, text search, category and verified-only filters, and sort by name/downloads/rating/latest. Install/Update/Uninstall run through mutations that invalidate the installed-connectors cache and surface sonner toasts; a ConnectorDetailModal and ConnectorInstallWizard drive details and installation.

user_story: As an operator, I want real-time system health and metrics so that I can spot degraded services and capacity trends quickly.
expected_behavior: Three polling queries: /cmdb-health/metrics (30s), /cmdb-health/services (10s), and /cmdb-health/timeseries?hours=N (60s) where N follows a 24h/90d/1y time-range toggle. Renders metric summary cards, a per-service status list color/icon-coded by status (healthy/degraded/down), and area/bar charts of CIs and anomalies over time, with manual refetch controls.

user_story: As an operator, I want to triage detected configuration anomalies so that I can investigate, resolve, or dismiss them.
expected_behavior: Loads anomaliesApi.getRecent(24,100) on a 30s auto-refresh plus anomaliesApi.getStats. Provides severity and status filters (status defaults to 'detected') applied client-side. An updateStatus mutation transitions an anomaly to investigating/resolved/false_positive (actor 'current_user'), shows a success/error toast, and invalidates the ['anomalies'] queries. Severity is rendered with color/icon helpers.

user_story: As an AI/automation engineer, I want to review and compile patterns learned from AI discoveries so that future discovery is faster and more accurate.
expected_behavior: Tabbed UI (Overview, Pattern Library, Discovery Sessions, Cost Analytics) rendering PatternLearningOverview, PatternLibrary, DiscoverySessionsView, and CostAnalyticsDashboard. A 'Compile Patterns' button calls compileAndSubmitPatterns() from useDiscoverySessions, disabling itself and showing a spinning icon while in flight.

user_story: As an executive, I want a high-level cost, health, and risk view so that I can understand the business impact of IT at a glance.
expected_behavior: Uses useExecutiveDashboard(timeRange) -> dashboardService.getExecutiveDashboard(days) (default 1y, 30s refresh) with an Alert on error and a loading guard. Defensively defaults serviceHealthByTier, riskMatrix.services, valueScorecard, costByCapability, costTrends, and topCostDrivers to empty, then computes an overall health score and high-risk service count; renders KPI cards, cost trend/breakdown charts, and a risk matrix. Export to PDF/Excel buttons call useExportDashboard (a stub).

user_story: As a CIO, I want technology portfolio and operational metrics so that I can steer investment and modernization decisions.
expected_behavior: Uses useCIODashboard(timeRange) -> dashboardService.getCIODashboard(days) (default 30d, 30s refresh), rendering an Alert on error and a loading state otherwise. Displays KPI cards, progress bars, and bar/pie/line charts built from the returned CIO metrics. Export PDF/Excel buttons invoke the useExportDashboard stub.

user_story: As an ITSM manager, I want incident, change, and SLA visibility so that I can manage service operations.
expected_behavior: Uses useITSMDashboard() -> dashboardService.getITSMDashboard() on a 10s refresh, splitting the response into openIncidents, changesInProgress, ciStatus, topFailingCIs, slaCompliance, and baselineCompliance slices. Renders KPI cards, an IncidentTable, and SLA/baseline compliance widgets; a priorityFilter state drives the IncidentTable. Export PDF/Excel buttons call the useExportDashboard stub.

user_story: As a FinOps analyst, I want cloud cost, budget, and optimization data so that I can control and reduce spend.
expected_behavior: Uses useFinOpsDashboard(timeRange) -> dashboardService.getFinOpsDashboard(days) (default 90d, 60s refresh), exposing cloudCosts, onPremVsCloud, costByTower, budgetVariance, unitEconomics, and costOptimization. Renders KPI cards plus cost-trend, cost-breakdown, and pie/bar charts, with an Alert on error and a loading spinner. Export PDF/Excel buttons call the useExportDashboard stub.

user_story: As a service owner, I want service health, revenue-at-risk, and dependency context so that I can protect critical business services.
expected_behavior: Uses useBusinessServiceDashboard(serviceId) -> dashboardService.getBusinessServiceDashboard(serviceId), returning serviceHealth, revenueAtRisk, customerImpact, complianceStatus, valueStreamHealth, and serviceDependencies. The query is enabled only when a service is selected (selectedService defaults to ''), so the dashboard stays empty until the user picks a service; selecting one renders KPIs and draws a cytoscape dependency graph into #dependency-graph. Export PDF/Excel buttons call the useExportDashboard stub.

user_story: As a service owner, I want to create and maintain business service records so that CIs can be mapped to the services they support.
expected_behavior: Loads business services through apiClient and maps API records to the UI shape via mapAPIToUI/mapUIToAPI. A create/edit Dialog captures name, criticality, status, and owner fields (criticality and status rendered with color badges), and supports deletion. Mutations surface success/error toasts via useToast.

user_story: As a finance/TBM analyst, I want IT spend, budget, and optimization views so that I can manage technology cost.
expected_behavior: Renders summary cards (monthly IT spend, budget utilization, potential savings, cost per user) and Overview/Cost by Tower/Service Costs/Optimization view tabs with recharts line, pie, and treemap visualizations. All figures come from hardcoded module-level constants (monthlyTrends, costByTower, businessServiceCosts, optimizationOpportunities); the time-range Select updates state but does not refetch since the data is static.
issue_note: The header 'Export Report' button (FinancialManagement.tsx:145) has no onClick handler, so it is a dead button; the page also renders only mock/hardcoded data with no backend fetch.

user_story: As an admin or user, I want to manage application preferences, profile, API keys, and integrations so that I can configure the platform.
expected_behavior: Tabbed UI synced to ?tab= (default 'general') rendering GeneralSettings, DiscoverySettings, NotificationSettings, UserProfile, ApiKeysManager, and DatabaseSettings. Admin-only tabs are gated behind hasRole(['admin']) (isAdmin). Each child component owns its own load/save logic.

user_story: As a user, I want to sign in so that I can access the protected CMDB application.
expected_behavior: Wrapped in PublicRoute; shows a 'Loading...' state while auth state resolves and redirects authenticated users to '/' via <Navigate>. Renders a branding panel and the LoginForm inside a LiquidGlass card; a 'Contact your administrator' link button is purely informational (no navigation/sign-up flow).

user_story: As a user, I want a top-bar search so that I can quickly jump to CIs by name, hostname, or IP from anywhere.
expected_behavior: On Enter, handleSearch trims the input and navigates to /cis?search=<encoded value> (or /cis when empty), letting the CIList page consume the search query param. The input is always visible in the sticky header.

user_story: As a multi-tenant user, I want to switch the active tenant so that I can view CMDB data for a different organization.
expected_behavior: Renders a pill button labeled 'Happy Technologies' with a buildings icon and a caret-down affordance suggesting a dropdown of tenants.
issue_note: The tenant switcher button (Header.tsx:71) has no onClick handler and no dropdown/menu wiring, so it is a dead control despite implying tenant switching.

user_story: As a user, I want a notifications button so that I can review alerts and system events.
expected_behavior: Renders a bell icon button in the sticky header with an aria-label of 'Notifications'.
issue_note: The notifications button (Header.tsx:102) has no onClick handler and no panel/badge wiring, so clicking it does nothing (dead button).

user_story: As an operator, I want at-a-glance service status in the header so that I know if the graph DB and data mart are healthy without opening the health page.
expected_behavior: Shares the ['service-health'] react-query cache with HealthDashboard, polling /cmdb-health/services every 10s. It finds the neo4j/graph and mart/warehouse services and shows pulsing status dots colored by status (healthy=success, degraded=warning, down=danger), falling back to a neutral dot with an 'unknown' tooltip when a service is missing.

user_story: As a user, I want a categorized navigation menu so that I can move between the dashboards, CMDB, discovery, integration, AI, and admin areas.
expected_behavior: Renders grouped nav categories (Configuration Plane, Business Insights, Service Management, CMDB, Discovery, Integration, AI & Analytics, Administration) as NavButtons that navigate via react-router and highlight the active route using useLocation. The 'Configuration Items' item shows a live CI count badge fetched through ciService. Supports permanent and temporary (Sheet drawer) variants.

user_story: As a stakeholder, I want to export a dashboard to PDF or Excel so that I can share or archive the reported metrics.
expected_behavior: Every Business Insights dashboard (Executive, CIO, ITSM, FinOps, Business Service) wires its Export PDF / Export Excel buttons to useExportDashboard's exportToPDF/exportToExcel.
issue_note: useExportDashboard is a placeholder (useDashboardData.ts:231-242): exportToPDF/exportToExcel only console.log and alert('PDF/Excel export will be implemented soon'), so all dashboard export buttons produce an alert instead of a file.

user_story: As an analyst, I want a tabbed analytics workspace so that I can review inventory, change, health, connection, relationship, and discovery statistics.
expected_behavior: Implements a full tabbed UI (Inventory, Changes, Health, Connections, Relationships, Discovery) with a DateRangeSelector feeding date-scoped child components (InventoryStats, ChangeTimeline, HealthMetrics, TopConnectedCIs, RelationshipMatrix, DiscoveryStats). The page is fully built but never mounted.
issue_note: pages/Analytics.tsx has no <Route> in App.tsx and is not imported anywhere, so the Analytics page is unreachable (dead code).

user_story: As a CMDB editor, I want a full inventory management page so that I can create, edit, delete, and view configuration items.
expected_behavior: Implements list/grid views via useCIs({limit:100}) and create/edit/delete dialogs through useCIActions (createCI/updateCI/deleteCI) with toasts and a CIForm; handleViewClick navigates to /inventory/:id. The page is fully implemented but never mounted.
issue_note: pages/Inventory.tsx has no <Route> in App.tsx and is not imported anywhere; this is why CIList's 'Manage CIs' and CIDetail's edit/delete navigations to /inventory dead-end at the Dashboard.

user_story: As an operator, I want to monitor background jobs, schedules, workers, and the dead-letter queue so that I can manage discovery and ETL processing.
expected_behavior: Implements a tabbed monitor (Active/Monitor, Completed, Failed, Schedules, Workers, Dead-letter) backed by useJobs, useQueueStats, useQueueHealth, and useSchedules across queues like discovery:aws and etl:sync, with JobMonitor/JobList/JobDetail/WorkerStatus/ScheduleList/DeadLetterQueue child components. The page is fully built but never mounted.
issue_note: pages/Jobs.tsx has no <Route> in App.tsx and is not imported anywhere, so the job-monitoring UI is unreachable (dead code).

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want a table of installed connectors so that I can update or uninstall them and see success rates.
expected_behavior: Loads connectorService.getInstalledConnectors() into a DataTable, computes per-connector success rate and available-update flags, and supports uninstall (AlertDialog confirm) and update actions with toasts. Fully implemented but never mounted.
issue_note: pages/connectors/InstalledConnectors.tsx has no <Route> and is not imported in App.tsx; it duplicates connector functionality already served by the routed pages/Connectors.tsx and pages/ConnectorCatalog.tsx, so it is dead code.

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want a detail page for a connector configuration so that I can run it, toggle it, view run history, and delete it.
expected_behavior: Reads :id from params, loads a ConnectorConfiguration via connectorService, and supports Run Now, enable/disable toggle, and delete (AlertDialog), plus a run-history DataTable and a recharts line chart of recent run metrics/success rate. Fully implemented but never mounted.
issue_note: pages/connectors/ConnectorConfigDetail.tsx has no <Route> (e.g. /connectors/:id) and is not imported in App.tsx, so this detail view is unreachable (dead code).

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want to browse and install connectors so that I can add integrations from the registry.
expected_behavior: Loads the registry and installed connectors via connectorService.getConnectorRegistry()/getInstalledConnectors(), supports search, category/verified/tag filters and clear-filters, marks already-installed connectors, and installs via a FormDialog with version selection. A second, older implementation of the catalog that is never mounted.
issue_note: pages/connectors/ConnectorCatalog.tsx has no <Route> and is not imported in App.tsx; the routed catalog is the top-level pages/ConnectorCatalog.tsx (/connectors/catalog), making this subdirectory file a dead duplicate.
